description: Write a function to find the maximum sum possible by using the given equation f(n) = max( (f(n/2) + f(n/3) + f(n/4) + f(n/5)), n).
code:
def get_max_sum (n):
	res = list()
	res.append(0)
	res.append(1)
	i = 2
	while i<n + 1:
		res.append(max(i, (res[int(i / 2)] 
						+ res[int(i / 3)] +
							res[int(i / 4)]
						+ res[int(i / 5)])))
		i = i + 1
	return res[n]
test_code:
assert get_max_sum(60) == 106
assert get_max_sum(10) == 12
assert get_max_sum(2) == 2

description: Write a function to compute the sum of digits of each number of a given list.
code:
def sum_of_digits(nums):
    return sum(int(el) for n in nums for el in str(n) if el.isdigit())
test_code:
assert sum_of_digits([10,2,56])==14
assert sum_of_digits([[10,20,4,5,'b',70,'a']])==19
assert sum_of_digits([10,20,-4,5,-70])==19

description: Write a function to check if a url is valid or not using regex.
code:
import re
def is_valid_URL(str):
	regex = ("((http|https)://)(www.)?" +
			"[a-zA-Z0-9@:%._\\+~#?&//=]" +
			"{2,256}\\.[a-z]" +
			"{2,6}\\b([-a-zA-Z0-9@:%" +
			"._\\+~#?&//=]*)")
	p = re.compile(regex)
	if (str == None):
		return False
	if(re.search(p, str)):
		return True
	else:
		return False
test_code:
assert is_valid_URL("https://www.google.com") == True
assert is_valid_URL("https:/www.gmail.com") == False
assert is_valid_URL("https:// www.redit.com") == False

code:
def round_and_sum(list1):
  lenght=len(list1)
  round_and_sum=sum(list(map(round,list1))* lenght)
  return round_and_sum

description: Write a function to find cubes of individual elements in a list using lambda function.
Write a function to calculate the sum of perrin numbers.
code:
def cal_sum(n): 
	a = 3
	b = 0
	c = 2
	if (n == 0): 
		return 3
	if (n == 1): 
		return 3
	if (n == 2): 
		return 5
	sum = 5
	while (n > 2): 
		d = a + b 
		sum = sum + d 
		a = b 
		b = c 
		c = d 
		n = n-1
	return sum
test_code:
assert cal_sum(9) == 49
assert cal_sum(10) == 66
assert cal_sum(11) == 88
